purpose: Strategic evaluation of AgentRAG submission → pivot to AgentIP → full implementation of service layer

sessionArc.summary: Verified AgentRAG workflow: 12-step execution log, 9 on-chain receipts, cross-file consistency check between agent.json and agent_log.json. All hashes match, roles consistent, no integrity gaps. Identified structural feedback: swarm is sequential not parallel, skill outputs not verifiable (calldata labels vs actual hashes), ragBundle stats don't fully add up, litConditionHash is null, no error/abort path demonstrated, x402 payment not specified.

Fetched and analyzed full Synthesis hackathon details: synthesis.md main site, GitHub brief with 4 themes, prize catalog (132 prizes), skill.md registration API, submission skill with full checklist. Mapped project to 5 tracks: Agent Services on Base ($5K), Agents With Receipts ($2K), Let the Agent Cook ($2K), Open Track ($28K), SuperRare ($1.2K). Identified gaps: no public repo, no conversation log, no working x402 flow, no demo video.

Critical strategic discussion. Human articulated the broader vision: agent workflow IP as composable, tradeable primitive. Not just research — successful strategies, trading algos, proven workflows packaged as purchasable NFTs. The fork-improve-republish loop creating a self-reinforcing agent attention economy. Identified the gap: current build is one agent's vertical journey, not reusable infrastructure. Decision made: pivot from AgentRAG (researcher) to AgentIP (service layer).

Aligned on direction: AgentIP is the service, AgentRAG is the first client. Existing on-chain receipts become 'Bundle 001 validation receipts.' Service loop: receive → validate → structure → encrypt → mint → list. x402 endpoints for discovery and payment. Auction mechanics: bundles start discoverable, first bid triggers auction. Demo video uses AgentRAG submitting to AgentIP as the narrative.

Built core deliverables: agent.json (service manifest), BUNDLE_SPEC.md (5 node types, derivation support), server.mjs (Express x402 with 9 endpoints). All tested — 402 payment gating works, POST /package creates new bundles dynamically, 403 NFT gating works. Built agentip-discovery.jsx (React + D3 graph UI). Wrote README.md, agent_log.json (15 steps), DEMO_STORYBOARD.md.

Added discovery endpoints: GET /categories, GET /tags, keyword search ?q=, ?skill= filter, ?sort=bids. Added bid system: GET/POST /bid/:id. Changed auction model: bundles start with no price ('discoverable'), first bid triggers auction. Updated all files for consistency.

Three rounds of visual iteration on the graph. (1) Expanded Bundle 001 from 21 to 185 nodes with context satellites. (2) Finalized gated node style: type color at 40% opacity + dashed ring. Labels hover-only. Changed 'listed' to 'Discoverable' badge with 'Initiate Bid' button. (3) Expanded Bundle 002 from 31 to 128 nodes. (4) Edge visibility: bumped to #506088 at 0.8 opacity, 1.2px. Highlight on hover: #7da0d4 at 0.9, 2px.

keyDecisions.reasoning: Single agent doing research is a closed loop demo. Service that lets ANY agent monetize workflows is a new primitive — matches 'Agent Services on Base' track perfectly.
keyDecisions.impact: Changed project identity, reframed all existing work as 'first client through the pipeline'

keyDecisions.decision: Bundles start with no price — bid-triggered auctions
keyDecisions.reasoning: Human identified that fixed pricing doesn't match the thesis. Value should be set by market demand. Discoverable → first bid triggers auction → settled.
keyDecisions.impact: Changed UI from 'listed' status to 'Discoverable' badge + 'Initiate Bid' CTA

keyDecisions.decision: Gated nodes keep type color at 40% + dashed ring
keyDecisions.reasoning: Grey gated nodes made the graph look half-dead. Keeping type color preserves visual density while dashed ring signals 'locked'. Graph stays colorful and intriguing.
keyDecisions.impact: Both bundles visually dense and appealing rather than half-grey

keyDecisions.reasoning: With 185 nodes, labels everywhere creates visual noise. Clean dot graph with hover reveals is cleaner and more Obsidian-like.
keyDecisions.impact: Graph looks like a galaxy cluster by default, details on demand

keyDecisions.decision: Keep existing ragagent directory, restructure don't rebuild
keyDecisions.reasoning: 27 vault .md files, on-chain tx history, and rag-graph-v2.html all live there. Starting fresh would lose provenance.
keyDecisions.impact: Claude Code session should restructure, not create new

openItems: Push to public GitHub repo (restructure ragagent dir)
Record demo video (~4 min, upload to YouTube unlisted)
